commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh For you have made him most blessed forever: you have made him exceedingly glad with your presence. Augustine of Hippo 7. "For Thou shalt give Him blessing for ever and ever. " This is the blessing which Thou shalt give Him for ever and ever: "Thou shall make Him glad in joy together with Thy countenance" (ver. According to His manhood, Thou shall make Him glad together with Thy countenance, which He lifted up to Thee.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
To be, (in benedictionem.) Thou shalt inebriate him with a torrent of thy blessings. (Haydock)
Blessing shall take hold of him, if we may so speak, (Berthier) and embrace him for ever. (Haydock)
All the saints receive glory. But Christ alone can impart it to others, as all are blessed in him, Genesis xxi., and John i. (Worthington)
When people wish any happiness to their friends, they need only desire that they may be like David. See Genesis xviii. 18., and xlviii. 20., and Galatians iii. 16. (Calmet)
